arts.ttl: Everton condemn online threats aimed at James Tarkowski
arts.sum: Everton have condemned the online abuse James Tarkowski has faced following Wednesday’s Merseyside Derby defeat to Liverpool. Tarkowski was fortunate to escape a red card following an 11th-minute challenge on Alexis Mac Allister. The centre-back won the ball but followed through in a reckless manner, catching Mac Allister high on the leg. He received a […] The post Everton condemn online threats aimed at James Tarkowski first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Arsenal believe 21-year-old Premier League player is edging closer to a move to Liverpool
arts.sum: Arsenal need to strengthen their squad in the summer transfer window if they want to get closer to Liverpool in the title race next season. The Gunners are expected to finish second for the third season in a row with another failure for Mikel Arteta’s side in the Premier League this season. arts.ttl: Man City launch new legal action claiming Premier League rivals like Arsenal have ‘unfair’ edge
arts.sum: Reigning Premier League champions Manchester City are set to go up against the league in relation to recent plans to amend rules around Associated Party Transactions

arts.ttl: Everton issue statement after James Tarkowski threats following tackle vs Liverpool
arts.sum: Everton have issued a statement condemning threats towards James Tarkowski after his tackle against Liverpool

arts.ttl: Everton condemn abuse of Tarkowski and family after wife posts of death threats
arts.sum: ‘Such behaviour is completely unacceptable,’ Everton say Samantha Tarkowski calls abuse ‘beyond disgusting’ Everton have strongly condemned abuse directed at their defender James Tarkowski after Wednesday’s Merseyside derby. The centre-back was targeted for his tackle on Alexis Mac Allister, which resulted in a yellow card but led to an acknowledgment from Premier Game Match Officials Ltd that the video assistant referee was wrong not to recommend an upgrade to a red. Tarkowski’s wife, Samantha, posted on Instagram on Thursday that they had received, among other things, death threats. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Viktor Gyokeres sends clear two-word message as Arsenal plan £62m transfer talks
arts.sum: In his last six games in all competitions, Gyokeres has now scored nine goals taking his tally for the season to a stunning 43 and with Andrea Berta a big fan, it is no surprise links to Arsenal have ramped up. Viktor Gyokeres has sent a clear message after scoring again for Sporting CP in a 2-0 Portuguese Cup victory over Rio Ave. The Sweden international endured a challenging February where his minutes were managed but he is now back firing at his very best.

arts.ttl: Everton condemn abuse directed at James Tarkowski following derby challenge
arts.sum: The centre-back was caught in the middle of a storm after his over-the-top tackle on Alexis Mac Allister resulted in only a yellow card.
